# Gate A/B examples — all 180 positions

For each position: the source context (text up to the sampled token), the AV
explanations of the activations at layers 18/20/22 (plus the independent
repeat at L20 — compare it with L20 to calibrate pure sampling noise), the
Claude diff labels for the real pair L18→L22 (v1 and delta-only v2 prompts)
and for the null pair L20 vs L20-repeat (same vector twice — any "difference"
is hallucinated), and the explanation the AV produced when the raw diff
vector v22−v18 was injected directly.


---

## Position 0 (doc 0, token 95: `'only'`)

**AV explanation, layer 18:**

> The phrase "I wish she'd at least *something* about pregnancy... If only" suggests an incomplete emotional clause expressing longing or wishful thinking, continuing the frustrated rant about the character's absent attributes or storylines.
> Final token "only" ends an incomplete clause ("If only"), part of a frustrated enumeration ("Even if only... Even if..."), expecting a noun or phrase like "to see it resolved" or "the protagonist could have been..." or "a tiny bit of happiness," completing the dreamy wishful thinking about the story.

> Fanfic blogger tone with sarcastic/angsty Twitter-style ramblings about a show character's pregnancy obsession, listing painful plot elements about the Eureka Three.
> The sentence structure "I wish Daisy would die or get better. Just pretend... Even if only" suggests an incomplete clause expressing frustrated hopes or longing, continuing sarcastic list or wishful thinking about story progression.
> Final token "only" ends an incomplete clause ("Even if only"), part of a multi-part frustrated wish ("If only... If only... I wish..."), strongly expecting a noun phrase like "she'd had a little action" or "to see a happy ending" or "the characters could understand."

**AV explanation, layer 20 (repeat — sampling-noise calibration):**
> Fanfic/TV blogger tone with informal angry rant about poorly written Heroes/Clark Kent story, listing frustrated plot points about Kara's pregnancy and lack of power.
> The sentence structure "I wish Kara would actually *do* something useful this season. Even if only" suggests a closing sarcastic remark or wishful thinking clause about fictional plot developments or character motivation.
> Final token "only" ends an incomplete clause ("Even if only... Even if only"), part of a frustrated enumeration of desired plot events ("If only... If only..."), strongly expecting "she would kill the boredom" or "a few minutes of her being remembered" or "I could believe the story."

**AV explanation, layer 22:**

> The sentence structure "I wish Becky and Katie would just get together again... Even if only for the plot... And if only" suggests an incomplete list of desires or regrets about Brenda's storyline, implying frustrated character developments or emotional arc.
> Final token "only" ends mid-clause ("If only... Even if only"), part of a quoted speculation clause ("I wish for some more Brenda's backstory... Even if only"), strongly anticipating a noun phrase like "to see some happiness" or "for a brief moment" or "she'd be redeemed."

**Claude diff v2 delta-only, L18→L22 (verdict: different):**
> Disney villain drama context with sarcastic humor, referencing fan theories about characters named Ava, Sarah, and Brenda rather than DC Comics Batman grievances.
> Character relationship dynamics involving Becky and Katie getting together, with focus on Brenda's backstory and redemption arc rather than complaints about a character's powerlessness.
> Casual fanfic writer tone anticipating romantic or plot-driven resolutions ("she'd be redeemed," "for a brief moment") rather than frustrated enumeration of absent attributes or storylines.
